metadata.subgroup_label: Logos — the word as inner-being engagement

verse_context_groups.label: The divine/apostolic word that enters and addresses the inner life
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names logos as the active divine or apostolic word that engages the inner person — the word of the cross that is folly or power depending on the inner condition of the hearer, the word of truth that produces salvation, the word of Christ dwelling richly within, the word of reconciliation entrusted to proclamation, the word of God whose integrity cannot fail. Inner-being phenomenon: the word as inner-life-addressing disclosure.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Col 3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly , teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom , singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs , with thankfulness in your hearts to God .
anchor_verse.meaning: logos names the word of Christ as the inner-dwelling presence — it takes up residence within the person richly, shaping inner wisdom and producing community formation. The word is a dwelling inner content.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly.' The logos as inner-dwelling presence is most clearly named here. The word of Christ is not merely heard or read — it takes up residence (oikeitō) within the person richly, shaping wisdom and producing community formation. The command assumes that the word has an inner location in the person, and that its richness of dwelling is something to be desired and cultivated.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:18 For the word of the cross is folly to those who are perishing , but to us who are being saved it is the power of God .
verses.meaning: logos names the word of the cross as an inner-differentiating reality — the same word produces opposite inner responses: folly to the perishing, power to the saved. The word addresses the inner life and reveals its condition.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:36 Or was it from you that the word of God came ? Or are you the only ones it has reached ?
verses.meaning: Names the divine word whose origin Paul questions — the term identifies the word of God as the active inner disclosure whose source the Corinthians are challenged to consider.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:2 and by which you are being saved , if you hold fast to the word I preached to you — unless you believed in vain .
verses.meaning: Names the proclaimed content by which the community is being saved — the term identifies the word as the inner message Paul preached, to which holding fast is necessary.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:54 When the perishable puts on the imperishable , and the mortal puts on immortality , then shall come to pass the saying that is written : “ Death is swallowed up in victory .”
verses.meaning: Names the saying written in Scripture — the term identifies the word as the scriptural utterance whose fulfilment will coincide with the final defeat of death.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:13 And we impart this in words not taught by human wisdom but taught by the Spirit , interpreting spiritual truths to those who are spiritual .
verses.meaning: logos names the Spirit-taught words that carry spiritual truth — inner wisdom expressed in word-form produced by the Spirit, not human inner wisdom.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:17 For we are not , like so many , peddlers of God’s word , but as men of sincerity , as commissioned by God , in the sight of God we speak in Christ .
verses.meaning: Names the divine word whose peddling Paul refuses — the term identifies the word of God as the sacred inner disclosure that is not to be traded as merchandise but spoken with sincerity.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:2 But we have renounced disgraceful , underhanded ways. We refuse to practice cunning or to tamper with God’s word , but by the open statement of the truth we would commend ourselves to everyone’s conscience in the sight of God .
verses.meaning: logos names the word of truth as the means of reaching the conscience — the open truthful word is directed at the inner conscience of every hearer. The logos functions as conscience-addressing word.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:19 that is , in Christ God was reconciling the world to himself , not counting their trespasses against them , and entrusting to us the message of reconciliation .
verses.meaning: Names the message entrusted to the apostles — the term identifies the word of reconciliation as an inner disclosure placed in their hands for proclamation to the world.

verses.verse_text: Col 1:25 of which I became a minister according to the stewardship from God that was given to me for you , to make the word of God fully known ,
verses.meaning: Names the content Paul was commissioned to make fully known — the term identifies the word of God as the inner disclosure entrusted to his stewardship.

verses.verse_text: Col 1:5 because of the hope laid up for you in heaven . Of this you have heard before in the word of the truth , the gospel ,
verses.meaning: Names the message heard in advance — the term identifies the word of truth as the inner content of the gospel that the recipients had already received.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly , teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom , singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs , with thankfulness in your hearts to God .
verses.meaning: logos names the word of Christ as the inner-dwelling presence — it takes up residence within the person richly, shaping inner wisdom and producing community formation. The word is a dwelling inner content.

verses.verse_text: Col 4:3 At the same time , pray also for us , that God may open to us a door for the word , to declare the mystery of Christ , on account of which I am in prison —
verses.meaning: Names the content for which an open door is sought — the term identifies the word as the inner disclosure concerning the mystery of Christ that Paul seeks opportunity to declare.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:13 In him you also , when you heard the word of truth , the gospel of your salvation , and believed in him , were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit ,
verses.meaning: Names the message by which salvation came — the term identifies the word of truth as the inner content of the gospel, upon hearing which the readers believed and were sealed.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:19 and also for me , that words may be given to me in opening my mouth boldly to proclaim the mystery of the gospel ,
verses.meaning: Names what Paul requests for himself — the term identifies words as the given capacity of inner expression he needs to proclaim the gospel boldly.

verses.verse_text: Gal 6:6 Let the one who is taught the word share all good things with the one who teaches .
verses.meaning: Names the content being taught — the term identifies the word as the inner disclosure communicated in instruction, whose reception creates a sharing obligation.

verses.verse_text: Rom 13:9 For the commandments, “You shall not commit adultery , You shall not murder , You shall not steal , You shall not covet ,” and any other commandment , are summed up in this word : “You shall love your neighbor as yourself .”
verses.meaning: logos names the summarising word that holds all commandments — the word as inner-dwelling moral principle. All the law's moral content is gathered into the single neighbour-love word, which becomes the internal moral standard.

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:28 for the Lord will carry out his sentence upon the earth fully and without delay .”
verses.meaning: Names the judicial sentence the Lord will execute — the term identifies the word as a decisive inner determination that will be carried out fully and swiftly on the earth.

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:6 But it is not as though the word of God has failed . For not all who are descended from Israel belong to Israel ,
verses.meaning: Names the divine word whose integrity is at stake — the term identifies the word of God as the active inner principle whose reliability Paul defends against the implication of failure.

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:9 For this is what the promise said : “ About this time next year I will return , and Sarah shall have a son .”
verses.meaning: Names the content of the promise — the term identifies the word as the vehicle of divine commitment, carrying the specific content of God's pledge to Abraham.

verse_context_groups.label: Human speech as communicative act evaluated by its inner quality
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names logos as the communicative act of the human person — evaluated by whether it is intelligible (reaching the inner person of the hearer), gracious and seasoned (shaping the encounter), consistent with deeds, or alternatively empty, arrogant, and inconsistent. The word-as-speech is the outward expression of the inner person and is evaluated by what inner reality it carries. Inner-being phenomenon: speech as the outward expression of inner quality.

anchor_verse.verse_text: 1Cor 14:19 Nevertheless , in church I would rather speak five words with my mind in order to instruct others , than ten thousand words in a tongue .
anchor_verse.meaning: logos names the words spoken with the mind (nous) — the inner-directed, intelligible word that reaches the inner person of the hearer and instructs. Quantity is irrelevant; the inner engagement of the mind is what counts.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'I would rather speak five words with my mind in order to instruct others, than ten thousand words in a tongue.' The phenomenon is explicit: words spoken with the nous (mind) reach the inner person of the hearer and instruct. Quantity is irrelevant; inner engagement of mind-to-mind is the criterion. The inner dimension of speech — the mind behind the word — determines whether it communicates.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:17 For Christ did not send me to baptize but to preach the gospel , and not with words of eloquent wisdom , lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.
verses.meaning: Names the rhetorical resource Paul was not sent to deploy — the term identifies the eloquence of wise speech as a human capacity whose use would empty the cross of its power.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:5 that in every way you were enriched in him in all speech and all knowledge —
verses.meaning: Names the capacity in which the community has been enriched — the term identifies word or speech as one of two inner gifts (alongside knowledge) bestowed through Christ.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:8 For to one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom , and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit ,
verses.meaning: Names one of the Spirit's gifts — the term identifies the utterance of wisdom as an inner capacity given to one member of the body for the community's benefit.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:19 Nevertheless , in church I would rather speak five words with my mind in order to instruct others , than ten thousand words in a tongue .
verses.meaning: logos names the words spoken with the mind (nous) — the inner-directed, intelligible word that reaches the inner person of the hearer and instructs. Quantity is irrelevant; the inner engagement of the mind is what counts.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:9 So with yourselves , if with your tongue you utter speech that is not intelligible , how will anyone know what is said ? For you will be speaking into the air .
verses.meaning: Names intelligible speech — the term identifies the utterance that communicates inner content as the prerequisite for understanding between speaker and hearer.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:1 And I , when I came to you , brothers , did not come proclaiming to you the testimony of God with lofty speech or wisdom .
verses.meaning: Names the mode Paul deliberately avoided — the term identifies lofty speech as a human rhetorical inner quality he set aside when he came to the Corinthians.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:4 and my speech and my message were not in plausible words of wisdom , but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power ,
verses.meaning: Names the quality Paul's speech and message lacked — the term identifies plausible words of wisdom as a rhetorical resource he consciously withheld.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:19 But I will come to you soon , if the Lord wills , and I will find out not the talk of these arrogant people but their power .
verses.meaning: logos names the talk that reveals the inner person's nature — the arrogant logos does not match the power that would evidence genuine inner life. The word is evaluated by what inner power it carries.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:20 For the kingdom of God does not consist in talk but in power .
verses.meaning: Names what the kingdom does not consist in — the term identifies talk or speech as a human inner activity that is insufficient to constitute God's kingdom.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:18 As surely as God is faithful , our word to you has not been Yes and No .
verses.meaning: Names the apostolic word whose integrity Paul defends — the term identifies the word as the personal commitment Paul and his co-workers have given, which has not wavered.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:10 For they say , “His letters are weighty and strong , but his bodily presence is weak , and his speech of no account .”
verses.meaning: Names the quality others attribute to Paul's letters — the term identifies the weight and force of his written word, which his critics contrast with his personal presence.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:11 Let such a person understand that what we say by letter when absent , we do when present .
verses.meaning: Names what Paul says by letter — the term identifies his spoken or written word as consistent with his actions, countering the charge of inconsistency.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:6 Even if I am unskilled in speaking , I am not so in knowledge ; indeed , in every way we have made this plain to you in all things .
verses.meaning: Names the capacity Paul claims despite his critics — the term identifies speech as the communicative inner ability in which he acknowledges deficiency, while asserting fullness in knowledge.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:7 by truthful speech , and the power of God ; with the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and for the left ;
verses.meaning: Names the means of truthful communication — the term identifies word alongside deed as the vehicle of authentic inner expression in the apostolic ministry.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 8:7 But as you excel in everything —in faith , in speech , in knowledge , in all earnestness , and in our love for you — see that you excel in this act of grace also .
verses.meaning: Names one of the capacities in which the community excels — the term identifies speech or word as an inner gift that Paul holds up alongside faith and knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Col 2:23 These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-made religion and asceticism and severity to the body , but they are of no value in stopping the indulgence of the flesh .
verses.meaning: Names the appearance of wisdom that certain practices carry — the term identifies word or discourse as the rhetorical form that makes self-imposed religion seem plausible.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:17 And whatever you do , in word or deed , do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus , giving thanks to God the Father through him .
verses.meaning: Names one mode of action to be done in the Lord's name — the term identifies word as the communicative dimension of human activity to be conducted under Christ's authority.

verses.verse_text: Col 4:6 Let your speech always be gracious , seasoned with salt , so that you may know how you ought to answer each person .
verses.meaning: Names the quality of speech Paul urges — the term identifies gracious and seasoned speech as an inner communicative quality that enables fitting answers.

verses.verse_text: Eph 4:29 Let no corrupting talk come out of your mouths , but only such as is good for building up , as fits the occasion , that it may give grace to those who hear .
verses.meaning: logos names speech evaluated by its inner moral quality and its effect on the hearer — the logos must carry grace, fit the occasion, and build up. The word is assessed by what it produces in the inner life of those who hear.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:6 Let no one deceive you with empty words , for because of these things the wrath of God comes upon the sons of disobedience .
verses.meaning: Names the instrument of deception to be resisted — the term identifies empty words as a hollow inner communication used to mislead, whose consequence is divine wrath.

verses.verse_text: Rom 15:18 For I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to bring the Gentiles to obedience —by word and deed ,
verses.meaning: Names the mode of Christ's work through Paul — the term identifies word as one of two means (alongside deed) through which the Gentiles are brought to obedience.

verse_context_groups.label: The word as summarising moral principle
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names logos as the single moral principle that holds and summarises all the law's content — the whole law is fulfilled in one word: love your neighbour. The logos here is not a speech-act or a divine disclosure but an inner moral principle of such compression and completeness that it contains all specific commandments. Inner-being phenomenon: the word as comprehensive inner moral standard.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Rom 14:12 So then each of us will give an account of himself to God .
anchor_verse.meaning: Names what each person must render — the term identifies the account or word as the personal disclosure that each will give before God.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'Any other commandment is summed up in this word: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.' The most explicit statement of the phenomenon: logos as the single inner moral principle that holds all commandments. The compression is deliberate — the word as moral principle contains all specific moral content within itself, making it the inner standard against which all particular acts are measured.

verses.verse_text: Gal 5:14 For the whole law is fulfilled in one word : “You shall love your neighbor as yourself .”
verses.meaning: logos names the single word that fulfils the entire law — the moral totality of the law compressed into neighbour-love. As in Rom 13:9, logos here is the inner moral principle.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:12 So then each of us will give an account of himself to God .
verses.meaning: Names what each person must render — the term identifies the account or word as the personal disclosure that each will give before God.

verse_context_groups.label: Set aside — no distinctive inner-being content in this span
verse_context_groups.context_description: Rom 3:4 — logos quotation of Psalm in rhetorical context; P-status noted in meaning.

verses.verse_text: Rom 3:4 By no means ! Let God be true though every one were a liar , as it is written , “ That you may be justified in your words , and prevail when you are judged .”
verses.meaning: logos P-status — quotation of Psalm, no distinctive inner-being content in span.
